items.title: Reach for `breakpoint()` before sprinkling prints
items.prefer: `breakpoint()` at the suspicious line; inspect everything you need in one pause.
items.why: Each print is one rerun; each breakpoint gives you the whole frame at once.
items.example: Inspect `messages`, `chunks`, `config`, and `len(text)` in one stop instead of four print-rerun cycles.

items.title: Use `ipdb` for tab completion + colours
items.why: Tab completion of attribute names is a force multiplier when exploring unfamiliar objects.
items.example: Type `client.<TAB>` to discover the API surface without leaving the prompt.

items.title: Lint against committed breakpoints
items.prefer: Enable a CI/pre-commit rule (ruff `T100`, flake8-debugger) that fails on `breakpoint()` / `pdb.set_trace()`.
items.why: An overlooked breakpoint hangs production the moment it's hit.

items.title: Prefer post-mortem to reproduction
items.avoid: Trying to engineer a minimal reproduction of a complex flake.
items.why: Post-mortem drops you exactly where the crash happened, with the real state intact.
items.example: `pytest --pdb -x` — first failure, into the debugger, inspect the offending fixture.

items.title: Treat the (Pdb) prompt as a scratchpad, not an editor
items.avoid: Mutating state at the prompt and forgetting it doesn't persist.
items.prefer: Use the prompt to *learn* what's wrong; apply the fix in source and rerun.
items.why: Sessions end; understanding is what you keep.
items.example: Discover that `client.base_url` is wrong → fix the config file → rerun.
